failureReasons: Plan specified 5 concrete changes: partition units, render unsupported-units banner, exclude unsupported from aggregates, state the denominator, and update badges. The diff only adds 3 struct fields (UnsupportedCount, UnsupportedLanguages, AnalyzableUnits) and a dead partitionByLanguage helper — none of these are called or rendered into the report card output.
Target file mismatch: plan said internal/report/report.go; change landed in internal/report/card.go.
The partitionByLanguage function splits on analysis.ForLanguage() != nil, but the plan specified Grade != "N/A" and Unsupported==true as criteria.

dimensions.evidence: Plan required 5 concrete changes (partition units, add banner, exclude from aggregates, state denominator, update badges). Diff only adds 3 struct fields and a dead helper function to card.go (not the specified report.go) — none of the 5 tasks are wired into actual report output.
Change is 19 lines in one file with no unrelated churn, but the plan explicitly targeted internal/report/report.go, and the diff landed in internal/report/card.go instead.

No tests were modified or weakened, but the diff does not actually fix the real defect — it adds unused struct fields and a dead partitionByLanguage function that is never called, so the failing behavior is not addressed.
New struct fields are backward-compatible and the unused function poses no regression risk, but advancing a build that leaves the plan almost entirely unimplemented is a correctness risk in itself.
